element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic melee combat foundation throughout game
Determines playstyle for entire run; Flight increases jump height, Life increases health, Spirit grants unlimited fireball
Alternative damage option; can be called to attack enemies
Found or purchased; expands combat options beyond melee
Resource management; purchase temporary power-ups (flight, life, spirit, fireballs) with gold from defeated enemies
Temporary replicas of crystal effects; collected from defeated enemies
Special item obtained by freeing the kidnapped Crystal Princess; represents progression milestone
Dropped by defeated enemies; enables shop purchases and progression strategy

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive difficulty escalation across five themed realms with boss encounters punctuating stage transitions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundational; introduces sword combat, crystal selection determines difficulty path, basic enemy patterns in Stage 1
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating; Stage 3 (Realm of Hungry Ghosts) noted as thematically intense with increased enemy density
difficulty_curve.late_game: Peak challenge; multiple perilous realms, boss encounters, Moon Mirror acquisition prerequisite adds progression gate
notable_spikes: Boss fights punctuating each realm transition
US version reduced overall difficulty compared to Japanese version

notable_level_patterns.description: Game structured as five distinct themed worlds, each with unique enemy types and environmental hazards. Story progression tied to conquering each realm sequentially
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 3 - Realm of Hungry Ghosts (complete aesthetic overhaul between JP/US versions)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage culminates in boss encounter that must be defeated to advance; boss victories mark narrative milestones in retaking Crystal Palace
Gold collected from enemies funds Kim's shop purchases; strategic resource management between combat and shop visits affects progression difficulty

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - gameplay mechanics, regional differences, enemy/stage changes
wikipedia - story structure, gameplay overview, crystal system, difficulty curve differences
